subject: GK_CurrentAffairs
content:
Q1. What is the name of the first Indian satellite?
Ans: Aryabhata
Q2. Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
Ans: Kosi
Q3. Who was the first woman President of India?
Ans: Pratibha Patil
Q4. What is the chemical symbol for gold?
Ans: Au
Q5. Which Mughal emperor built the Taj Mahal?
Ans: Shah Jahan
Q6. What is the largest planet in our solar system?
Ans: Jupiter
Q7. Who wrote the Indian National Anthem, 'Jana Gana Mana'?
Ans: Rabindranath Tagore
Q8. What is the capital of Rajasthan?
Ans: Jaipur
Q9. Which Indian state is known as the 'Spice Garden of India'?
Ans: Kerala
Q10. Who is known as the 'Iron Man of India'?
Ans: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
Q11. What is the full form of ISRO?
Ans: Indian Space Research Organisation
Q12. Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
Ans: Nitrogen
Q13. What is the name of the highest mountain peak in India?
Ans: Kanchenjunga
Q14. Who invented the polio vaccine?
Ans: Jonas Salk
Q15. Which city is known as the 'Pink City' of India?
Ans: Jaipur
Q16. What is the currency of Japan?
Ans: Yen
Q17. Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
Ans: Jawaharlal Nehru
Q18. What is the name of the Indian Parliament?
Ans: Sansad
Q19. In which year did India gain independence?
Ans: 1947
Q20. What is the study of earthquakes called?
Ans: Seismology

subject: GK_CurrentAffairs
content:
Q1. Which Mughal emperor built the Jama Masjid in Delhi?
Ans: Shah Jahan
Q2. What is the name of the longest river in India?
Ans: Ganga
Q3. Who is the author of the book "India Wins Freedom"?
Ans: Abul Kalam Azad

Ans: Article 17
Q5. What is the chemical symbol for gold?
Ans: Au
Q6. Which Indian state is known as the "Spice Garden of India"?
Ans: Kerala
Q7. Who was the first woman Prime Minister of India?
Ans: Indira Gandhi
Q8. What is the name of the Indian Space Research Organisation's (ISRO) lunar mission launched in 2008?
Ans: Chandrayaan-1
Q9. Which city is known as the "Electronic City of India"?
Ans: Bangalore
Q10. What is the name of the highest civilian award in India?
Ans: Bharat Ratna
Q11. Who invented the Polio vaccine?
Ans: Jonas Salk
Q12. Which Indian state has the largest coastline?
Ans: Gujarat
Q13. Who was the first President of the Indian National Congress?
Ans: Womesh Chandra Bonnerjee
Q14. What is the full form of AIDS?
Ans: Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome
Q15. Which planet is known as the "Red Planet"?
Ans: Mars
Q16. What is the name of the currency of Japan?
Ans: Yen
Q17. Who wrote the Indian National Anthem, "Jana Gana Mana"?
Ans: Rabindranath Tagore
Q18. What is the name of the strait separating India and Sri Lanka?
Ans: Palk Strait
Q19. Which gas is commonly known as laughing gas?
Ans: Nitrous Oxide
Q20. Who is known as the "Iron Man of India"?
Ans: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
